title: T? nullability
manager: `T?` as concise syntax for "a T or null," for the pragmatic cases where an optional field reads more naturally as a nullable than as a full `option<T>`. It keeps the null/undefined distinction honest while giving a lightweight spelling for the very common "might be absent" shape, and the checker still forces you to handle the null branch before you use the value.
cs: `T?` nullable type sugar; interacts with null/undefined (W6.12) and option (W6.22); checker enforces the null-handling branch.
